Murielle, one of our best analysts wants to leave. What should we do?
We should talk to her first and understand why. It is advisable to listen before we act.
And if it is about money?
We could offer a raise, or we could propose a new role. Both are worthwhile options.
The main drawback is cost, but the downside of losing her is worse.
We should weigh up both options. She might stay if we act quickly and are cautious.
She might stay if we act quickly.
We should weigh up both options.

Hello. Let me share some advice on our staffing dilemma. First, you should not panic. It is advisable to look at all the options calmly. You could offer flexible hours, or you might introduce a mentoring program. Both are feasible and worthwhile. Of course, every option has a drawback, so you should weigh up the costs and benefits. I would be cautious about a quick decision. The likelihood of keeping our best people is much higher if we listen first. In short, you should act, but you should act carefully.

Good morning. Let me talk through our options for the new policy. We could introduce it immediately, but that might cause resistance. Alternatively, we could test it with one team first. That is a more cautious and feasible approach. The main downside is that it takes longer. However, the likelihood of success is higher, and the drawback is small. It is advisable to weigh up both paths. I think a tentative rollout is worthwhile. We should decide this week, but we should not rush the details. Thank you.
